home.testimonial_3_text: A magnificent setting and refined cuisine. We always come back with pleasure.

about.title: About
about.hero_subtitle: Since 2009, we elevate the flavors of Madagascar.
about.history_text: Founded in 2009 by Chef Andry Rakoto, Le Gourmet was born from a passion: to marry French gastronomy with the authentic flavors of Madagascar. Located in the heart of Antananarivo, our restaurant offers refined cuisine in an elegant and warm setting. Each dish tells the story of an exceptional terroir, enhanced by the know-how of our brigade.
about.restaurant_desc: Nestled in the heart of Antananarivo, Le Gourmet welcomes you in an elegant yet warm atmosphere. Our main dining room, bathed in natural light, can seat up to 60 guests. Local stone walls, exposed beams, and Malagasy artworks create a unique atmosphere where tradition and modernity meet. Our shaded terrace, surrounded by exotic plants, is the perfect spot for a peaceful lunch or a candlelit dinner under the Tananarive stars.
about.value_1_desc: We work exclusively with local producers to guarantee freshness and authenticity.
about.value_2_desc: Each dish is a work of art, prepared with passion and precision by our brigade.
about.value_3_desc: Warm welcome and attentive service are at the heart of our philosophy.
about.value_4_desc: We adopt eco-friendly practices to preserve our beautiful island.
about.value_5_desc: Strong partnerships with our communities for lasting positive impact.
about.value_6_desc: An unwavering commitment to excellence at every level.
about.value_7_desc: Love for the craft guides every one of our actions.

blog.title: Blog
blog.post_1_excerpt: Discover why Bourbon vanilla is considered the best in the world.
blog.post_1_body: Madagascar vanilla, also known as Bourbon vanilla, is renowned worldwide for its exceptional quality. Grown mainly in the SAVA region in northeastern Madagascar, this vanilla owes its unique character to the volcanic soil and tropical climate of the island. The manual pollination process, inherited from the colonial era, is still practiced today by local producers. After nine months of maturation, the pods are carefully harvested and then sun-dried for several weeks. This artisanal process gives Malagasy vanilla its complex aromas of caramel, chocolate, and dried fruits. At Le Gourmet, we select our pods directly from producers in the Antalaha region, guaranteeing incomparable traceability and freshness.
blog.post_2_title: Recipe: Our Grilled Zebu at Home
blog.post_2_excerpt: Chef Andry reveals the secrets of his signature grilled zebu.
blog.post_2_body: Grilled zebu is one of the most emblematic dishes of Madagascar, and at Le Gourmet we have given it a gourmet dimension. To make this dish at home, start by choosing a quality zebu fillet, ideally aged for 21 days. Marinate the meat for four hours in a mixture of olive oil, crushed garlic, fresh thyme, and black pepper from Madagascar. Sear the fillet over high heat in a cast-iron pan for three minutes on each side, then finish cooking in the oven at 180°C for eight to ten minutes. Let the meat rest for five minutes before slicing. Serve with a Voatsiperifery pepper sauce, a wild berry endemic to Madagascar with woody and fruity notes. Accompany with roasted vegetables and fragrant rice from the Alaotra region.
blog.post_3_excerpt: An overview of the spices that make Malagasy cuisine so rich.
blog.post_3_body: Madagascar is a true paradise of spices. Besides vanilla, the island produces some of the finest spices in the world. Voatsiperifery pepper, a wild berry harvested in the eastern forests, offers woody and fruity notes that are incomparable. Cloves from Madagascar, originally from the Toamasina region, are recognized for their high eugenol content. Cinnamon from the island, milder than its Asian counterpart, comes from the Diana region. Fresh turmeric, grown in the highlands, is used in many traditional dishes. Finally, Malagasy ginger, particularly aromatic, is an essential ingredient in our cuisine. Each spice tells the story of a unique terroir, shaped by the tropical climate and volcanic soils of the island.
blog.post_4_excerpt: How to pair French wines with the flavors of Madagascar.
blog.post_4_body: The alliance of French wine and Malagasy cuisine is a fascinating culinary adventure. To accompany grilled zebu, we recommend a red Bordeaux from the right bank, such as a Saint-Émilion, whose silky tannins harmonize perfectly with the meat. Seafood dishes, like our vanilla lobster, pair wonderfully with a white Sancerre or a premier cru Chablis. For vanilla desserts, a Muscat de Beaumes-de-Venise or a natural sweet wine from the Rhône valley brings perfect roundness. Chef Andry and our sommelier Luc Nirina have developed a wine list specially designed to enhance each dish on our menu. Feel free to ask our team for advice during your visit.
blog.post_5_excerpt: Discover our chef's new dishes for the season.
blog.post_5_body: Spring is our favorite season at Le Gourmet. It's when Madagascar's fresh produce is most abundant and flavorful. Our new menu highlights early vegetables from the highlands, seasonal fish from Nosy Be, and juicy citrus from the Antananarivo region. Among the new dishes, discover our tuna tartare with green mango and passion fruit vinaigrette, a light and tangy dish that awakens the palate. For the main course, the roasted line-caught sea bass with sweet potato purée and combava beurre blanc is a true revelation. For desserts, the thin strawberry tart from Madagascar with vanilla cream is a tribute to local products. Chef Andry and his team worked for weeks to create this menu celebrating the renewal of the Malagasy spring.
blog.post_6_excerpt: The secrets of our homemade infused rums, an ancestral know-how.
blog.post_6_body: Infused rum is a Malagasy tradition that goes back several generations. At Le Gourmet, we perpetuate this art with passion. Our selection of homemade infused rums is carefully prepared by our head sommelier. Each bottle is unique: we use agricultural white rum from Madagascar that we let macerate for three to six months with carefully selected ingredients. Our Bourbon vanilla rum is the most emblematic, with its sweet and caramelized notes. The lychee rum, more fruity, is perfect as an aperitif. The mango and ginger rum offers a subtle balance between sweetness and spiciness. Finally, our cocoa and cinnamon rum is an exceptional digestif. These rums are available on the menu and for takeaway in our shop.

contact.title: Contact
contact.hero_subtitle: We would be delighted to welcome you.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: Do I need to book in advance?
faq.a1: We recommend booking at least 24 hours in advance, especially on weekends. For groups of more than 6 people, 48 hours are advised.
faq.a2: Yes, we offer vegetarian and vegan options on request. Don't hesitate to inform us of your preferences when booking.
faq.a3: We are open from Tuesday to Sunday: lunch from 12pm to 2:30pm and dinner from 7pm to 10:30pm. Closed on Mondays.
faq.a4: Yes, our private room can accommodate up to 30 people for your events: birthdays, business meals, celebrations.
faq.a5: We accept Visa and Mastercard cards as well as payments in Ariary and euros.

team.title: Chef & Team
team.hero_subtitle: Passionate gastronomy professionals at your service.
team.coworking_desc: At Le Gourmet, we believe that an exceptional workspace is the key to exceptional cuisine. Our modern kitchen and elegant dining room provide our team with an environment where creativity and excellence flourish every day.

legal.title: Legal Notice
legal.section_1_text: This site is published by Le Gourmet SARL, registered with the RCS of Antananarivo under number 2009B54321. The registered office is located at 12 Rue Colbert, Antananarivo 101. The publication director is Andry Rakoto.
legal.section_2_text: Use of this site implies acceptance of the terms of use. Data collected through reservation forms is processed in accordance with GDPR.
legal.section_3_text: Information collected through our forms is exclusively intended for our team for reservation management. We do not sell or share your data. In accordance with GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify and delete your data.
legal.section_4_text: This site uses only essential technical cookies and anonymized analytical cookies. No advertising tracking cookies are placed.
